methodology_sections: 2.3 Data Flow Tracing - Input flow, external data, asset movement, permission escalation
3.2 Business Logic Analysis - Economic incentives, edge cases, assumption violations, race conditions

data_flows.source: User (msg.sender with ERC20 approval or ETH value)
transformations: safeTransferFrom measures actual received amount via balance delta for fee-on-transfer tokens
data_flows.trust_changes: User input becomes trusted vault allowance entry; Bifrost observes Deposit event
exposure_risks: Any address can call depositWithExpiry emitting Deposit events with arbitrary memo to trick Bifrost
Deposit event amount is based on actual received amount (correct)
manipulation_risks: Log spoofing: anyone can emit Deposit events with crafted memos to any vault address for dust amounts
Bifrost must filter deposits below dust threshold and validate vault addresses

transformations: No fee-on-transfer accounting on outbound; emitted amount equals requested amount, not actual received
sinks: ETH -> recipient or bounced back to vault
data_flows.trust_changes: Vault allowance (trusted) is decremented; tokens leave router custody
validation_points: Solidity 0.8 underflow check on allowance subtraction
exposure_risks: For fee-on-transfer tokens, emitted TransferOut event amount differs from actual recipient amount, causing Bifrost accounting discrepancy
manipulation_risks: If vault has allowance, any EOA calling as that vault address can drain tokens - but vaults are THORChain-controlled multisig addresses

transformations: msg.value used directly, fromAmount parameter from struct is passed in call data but not used for value transfer
sinks: Success: aggregator performs swap, tokens to recipient
Fail: ETH to aggregator target (NOT recipient)
Fail2: ETH bounced back to vault
data_flows.trust_changes: ETH transferred to untrusted aggregator; if swap fails, ETH sent to aggregator target, not intended recipient
exposure_risks: Fallback sends ETH to aggregator target instead of recipient - user may lose funds if aggregator cannot return them
In batch mode, msg.value is reused for every ETH-based call
manipulation_risks: batchTransferOutAndCallV5 with multiple ETH items: each call sends full msg.value to aggregator, first call drains it, subsequent calls send 0 ETH (or revert on fallback)

transformations: Allowance decremented, ERC20 transferred to aggregator, then aggregator called
sinks: Success: aggregator swaps tokens to recipient
Fail: ERC20 tokens remain stuck in aggregator contract
data_flows.trust_changes: Router custody -> aggregator custody (no recovery mechanism if swap fails)
exposure_risks: If aggregator swap fails, ERC20 tokens are irrecoverably sent to aggregator with no fallback mechanism
Tokens stuck in aggregator depend on aggregator having rescue/recovery functions
manipulation_risks: Malicious or buggy aggregator could receive tokens, fail the swap intentionally, and keep the tokens via rescueFunds

path: Different router: _routerDeposit(router, newVault, asset, amount, memo)
transformations: Allowance moved between vaults in same router, or tokens approved and deposited into new router
sinks: Different router: tokens transferred via depositWithExpiry on new router
data_flows.trust_changes: Vault A loses allowance, Vault B gains allowance; for cross-router: tokens leave current router
validation_points: safeApprove does not reset to 0 first (USDT incompatibility)
exposure_risks: _routerDeposit calls safeApprove without resetting to 0 first, fails for USDT-like tokens with approve race protection
manipulation_risks: If _routerDeposit partially fails after approval but before depositWithExpiry, stale approval remains on new router

path: safeApprove(token, swapRouter, amount) // uses original amount, not _safeAmount
transformations: Token -> ETH via DEX swap -> deposit into THORChain router
sinks: ETH deposited to THORChain vault via router
data_flows.trust_changes: User token -> aggregator -> DEX -> ETH -> THORChain router -> vault
exposure_risks: safeApprove uses original amount not _safeAmount: over-approves for fee-on-transfer tokens
address(this).balance includes any pre-existing ETH in aggregator, not just swap output
manipulation_risks: An attacker can send dust ETH to aggregator to inflate the deposit amount observed by next swapIn caller

transformations: Transfers from tx.origin balance, bypassing allowance system
data_flows.trust_changes: Any contract in the call chain can transfer from the original transaction signer
validation_points: Only balance check, no approval check
exposure_risks: Phishing attack: malicious contract can steal all ETH.RUNE from tx.origin by being called in any transaction
manipulation_risks: If user interacts with any malicious contract, that contract can call transferTo to drain user's ETH.RUNE balance

path: Same router: loop _adjustAllowances for each coin
Different router: loop _routerDeposit for each coin
sinks: Allowances moved to asgard vault, ETH sent to asgard
data_flows.trust_changes: Old vault loses all allowances, new asgard gains them
exposure_risks: If asgard is a contract that rejects ETH, the entire returnVaultAssets call reverts, preventing vault migration
manipulation_risks: No validation that coins array matches actual vault holdings; vault must have sufficient allowance for each coin

core_rules: Vault allowance tracks ERC20 custody per vault; ETH is forwarded directly and not tracked in allowances
Only the vault address (msg.sender) can spend its allowance via transferOut/transferOutAndCall
Deposits are permissionless - anyone can deposit to any vault with any memo
Aggregator whitelist is enforced off-chain by Bifrost/THORNode, not on-chain by the router
NonReentrant modifier protects all state-changing functions
Fee-on-transfer tokens are handled on inbound (deposit) via balance-diff, but NOT on outbound (transferOut)
economic_incentives: Users deposit to initiate cross-chain swaps, incentivized by exchange rates
Vaults are incentivized to process outbound transfers to earn rewards from THORChain
Aggregators earn fees from DEX swaps on the final mile
No on-chain fees in the router itself - all fee logic is on THORChain L1
governance_mechanisms: Vault rotation (churn) managed by THORChain consensus, executed via transferAllowance
Aggregator whitelist managed off-chain by THORNode - fuzzy matching on last 3 hex digits
Router upgrades via returnVaultAssets + _routerDeposit to new router

edge_cases.scenario: batchTransferOutAndCallV5 with multiple ETH-based (fromAsset=address(0)) aggregation payloads
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Each payload should use its own fromAmount of ETH for the aggregator swap
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Each _transferOutAndCallV5 call uses msg.value (total ETH sent with batch tx), not aggregationPayload.fromAmount. First call forwards all ETH; subsequent calls forward msg.value again but the router may not have enough ETH, causing fallback cascades
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: If THORChain nodes construct a batch with multiple ETH-based aggregation calls, the first call receives all ETH and subsequent calls fail. ETH may be stuck in aggregator or bounced back incorrectly. In practice, nodes likely only include one ETH call per batch, but the code is incorrect.

edge_cases.scenario: _transferOutAndCallV5 ETH path fallback sends to aggregator target instead of recipient
edge_cases.expected_behavior: If aggregator swap fails, ETH should be sent to the intended recipient (like V4 transferOutAndCall does)
edge_cases.actual_behavior: ETH is sent to aggregationPayload.target (the aggregator contract), not aggregationPayload.recipient
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: If the aggregator's swapOutV5 fails, ETH is sent to the aggregator contract. If aggregator has no withdrawal mechanism for arbitrary ETH, funds are stuck. Differs from V4 behavior where fallback sends to `to` (recipient).

edge_cases.scenario: _transferOutAndCallV5 ERC20 path: aggregator swapOutV5 call fails
edge_cases.expected_behavior: ERC20 tokens should be recoverable by the recipient or returned to the router
edge_cases.actual_behavior: ERC20 tokens are already transferred to the aggregator. If swapOutV5 fails, tokens are stuck in the aggregator with no recovery mechanism in the router. The transaction still succeeds (by design).
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: A malicious or buggy aggregator could intentionally fail the swap and keep the tokens. Recovery depends on the aggregator having a rescueFunds function accessible to the owner.

edge_cases.expected_behavior: safeApprove should successfully approve the new router to spend USDT
edge_cases.actual_behavior: USDT requires approve to be set to 0 before setting a non-zero value. safeApprove in _routerDeposit does not reset to 0 first, so if any stale allowance exists, the approve call will fail and revert the migration
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Router migration for USDT could be blocked if there's any stale approval to the new router address. This could be triggered by a griefing attack where someone front-runs with a tiny approval.

edge_cases.scenario: Fee-on-transfer token outbound transfer via transferOut
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Event should emit actual amount received by recipient, and allowance should be reduced by actual transfer cost
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Allowance is reduced by full amount and event emits full amount, but recipient receives less due to transfer fee. Bifrost may record incorrect outbound amount.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Accounting discrepancy between on-chain actual transfers and Bifrost-observed amounts for fee-on-transfer tokens. Could lead to THORChain L1 state inconsistency.

edge_cases.scenario: transferOut ETH to a contract that reverts on receive (RevertingContract)
edge_cases.expected_behavior: ETH should be bounced back to the vault
edge_cases.actual_behavior: to.send() returns false, then msg.sender.transfer(safeAmount) is called. ETH is returned to vault. Event emits the full amount with the recipient address, even though recipient never received funds.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Bifrost observes TransferOut event with amount but recipient never received ETH. Could cause THORChain to mark outbound as complete when it wasn't delivered.

edge_cases.scenario: ETH_RUNE transferTo called by a malicious contract in any user transaction
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Only msg.sender should be able to transfer their tokens
edge_cases.actual_behavior: transferTo uses tx.origin, so any contract called by the user can drain their ETH.RUNE
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Phishing attack: deploy a contract that calls ETH_RUNE.transferTo(attacker, victim_balance) when any user interacts with it. All of the user's ETH.RUNE is stolen.

edge_cases.scenario: Aggregator swapIn with pre-existing ETH balance in aggregator
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Only ETH from the current swap should be deposited to THORChain
edge_cases.actual_behavior: address(this).balance is used after swap, which includes any pre-existing ETH in the aggregator (from previous failed swaps, direct sends, etc.)
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Attacker sends dust ETH to aggregator to inflate the deposit amount for the next swapIn caller, or steal pre-existing ETH by calling swapIn at the right time.

edge_cases.actual_behavior: _vaultAllowance[msg.sender][asset] -= 0 succeeds, transfer(to, 0) succeeds, event emitted with amount=0
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Event spam: vault can emit many TransferOut events with 0 amount to create noise for Bifrost observers

edge_cases.scenario: depositWithExpiry with vault = address(0) and asset = address(0)
edge_cases.actual_behavior: vault.send(msg.value) with vault = address(0) will fail (send to zero address), require(success) reverts

assumption_violations: Assumption: msg.value equals fromAmount in _transferOutAndCallV5 ETH path - not enforced, breaks in batch mode
Assumption: aggregator will always forward or return tokens on failure - no guarantee, ERC20 can be stuck
Assumption: approve(spender, amount) works for all tokens - USDT requires reset to 0 first
Assumption: transfer() returns bool or nothing - correct for most tokens but StealToken shows side-effect risk during transfer callbacks
Assumption: vault addresses are valid THORChain vaults - not validated on-chain, any address accepted
Assumption: aggregator swap failure is recoverable for ETH - V5 sends to target not recipient, inconsistent with V4
game_theory_risks: Log spoofing: attacker deposits dust amounts with crafted memos to trick Bifrost into processing fake swaps
Aggregator front-running: aggregator or MEV bot can sandwich swap transactions for profit
Vault impersonation: if an attacker somehow obtains vault address's private key, they can drain all allowances
Griefing returnVaultAssets: if asgard address is a contract that conditionally reverts, vault migration is blocked
integration_risks: USDT approve race condition breaks _routerDeposit for router migration
Fee-on-transfer tokens cause accounting discrepancies between router events and actual transfers
Rebasing tokens (if used) would break the allowance accounting since balances change without transfers
Pausable tokens (like USDT) could block outbound transfers if paused, locking funds in router
Blacklistable tokens (like USDT/USDC) could blacklist the router address, locking all custodied tokens
Non-standard ERC20s that don't revert on failure but return false are handled correctly by the low-level call pattern
External router in _routerDeposit is an untrusted contract - could be malicious and steal approved tokens without completing deposit
calculation_errors: THORChain_Aggregator.swapIn: safeApprove(token, swapRouter, amount) approves original amount instead of _safeAmount for fee-on-transfer tokens - over-approval but not loss
_transferOutAndCallV5 ETH path: uses msg.value instead of aggregationPayload.fromAmount for value transfer - mismatch in batch operations
No calculation error in allowance arithmetic - all use Solidity 0.8 checked math

token_semantics.notes: approve() requires current allowance to be 0 before setting non-zero value. transfer/transferFrom do not return bool. Has basisPointsRate fee. Router safeTransferFrom handles correctly on inbound, but _routerDeposit safeApprove may fail due to approve race condition. Pausable and blacklistable can lock router funds.
Contains transferTo(recipient, amount) using tx.origin - critical phishing vulnerability. Owner can mint up to 500M maxSupply. Intended as transitionary asset.
Standard WETH9 implementation. transferFrom allows infinite approval optimization.
Standard test ERC20 with burn. No special behaviors.

Malicious test token: transfer() calls _steal() which attempts to transfer a target token. Attack vector: if router calls transfer on StealToken, the steal attempt calls target.transfer from StealToken (not router), so router funds are safe due to msg.sender being StealToken not Router.
Malicious test token: transferFrom() attempts reentrancy by calling iROUTER.deposit on first call. Router's nonReentrant modifier prevents this attack.
Native asset represented as address(0). Forwarded directly to vault on deposit, not tracked in allowance system. Sent via .send() with 2300 gas stipend.

invariants.name: vault_allowance_conservation
invariants.description: Sum of all _vaultAllowance[*][asset] should equal Router's balanceOf(asset) for any given ERC20 asset. Violated if fee-on-transfer tokens are transferred out (allowance decremented by nominal amount, but actual balance reduced by nominal + fee).

invariants.name: allowance_transfer_conservation
invariants.description: In _adjustAllowances: decrease in old vault equals increase in new vault. Solidity 0.8 checked math prevents underflow.

invariants.name: eth_not_tracked_in_allowance
invariants.description: ETH (address(0)) is never tracked in vault allowance. All ETH deposits are forwarded directly to vault. _vaultAllowance[vault][address(0)] should always be 0.

invariants.name: reentrancy_guard
invariants.description: All public state-changing functions protected by nonReentrant modifier. _status transitions: _NOT_ENTERED -> _ENTERED -> _NOT_ENTERED.
